com.mentor.chs.plugin.constraint

Interface IXModuleAssignationConstraint<XMC extends IXModuleCode>

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXConstraint, IXPlugin
    All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXFunctionalModuleAssignationConstraint, IXProductionModuleAssignationConstraint

    @NotToBeImplementedDirectly
public interface IXModuleAssignationConstraint<XMC extends IXModuleCode>
extends IXConstraint
    Custom Constraint allows control of how a specific set of selected objects, or all objects in the current design should be assigned module codes as part of the Capital ModularXC assignation action.

    Three types of assignation are possible.

    Single object assignation - IXModuleAssignationObjectAlgorithmConstraint
    Single object technical assignation - IXModuleAssignationTechnicalConstraint
    Design / Multiple Object assignation - IXModuleAssignationConstraint

    Plugins implementing this constraint allow the user to analyze a set of selected objects, or the entire current design in order to determine the required module codes. This constraint is performed once per assignation run. It is the resposibility of the plugin to analyze the selected objects or design in the required order.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      boolean assignModuleCodes(java.util.Set<IXModuledObject> objects, IXHarnessDesign design, IXModuleAssignationResult<XMC> result)
      Perform module assignation on the given object set / design

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXPlugin

        getDescription, getName, getVersion

    • Method Detail

      • assignModuleCodes

        boolean assignModuleCodes(java.util.Set<IXModuledObject> objects,
                          IXHarnessDesign design,
                          IXModuleAssignationResult<XMC> result)
        Perform module assignation on the given object set / design
        Parameters:
        objects - list of objects for assignation - may be null
        design - harness design for assignation - may be null
        result - the result object to be populated
        Returns:
        true if module codes should be assigned to all objects specified in the result set. Where this method returns false, none of the specified objects will be assigned module codes, and the entire set of objects will be considered for assignation by any additional plugins during the same assignation run.
